channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: UN calls for reparations to remedy the 'historical wrongs' of trafficking enslaved Africans
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.N. General Assembly adopted a resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans "the gravest crime against humanity" and calling for reparations.

(Image credit: Pamela Smith)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: State-of-the-art air defense systems have not been able to stop some Iranian missiles
item.description: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. and Israel say they've depleted most of Iran's missile arsenal, but its weapons — including controversial cluster munitions — are challenging even the most advanced air-defense systems.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Estrin

item.title: Sweden's top general says U.S. remains 'really important ally'
item.description: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with Sweden's chief of defense, Gen. Michael Claesson, about NATO, the wars in Ukraine and Iran, and Europe's relationship with the United States.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zamora

item.title: Greetings from Turkey's border with Iran, where Iranians let loose on the dance floor
item.description: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.
item.content:encoded: Iranians escaping hardship and war are shaking it off to Persian, Arabic and Turkish tunes in this disco in eastern Turkey.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: For the first time in more than 1,400 years, Church of England gets a woman leader
item.description: A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.
item.content:encoded:
A new archbishop of Canterbury has been installed in a historic ceremony. Sarah Mullally is the 106th person to hold the job, and the first woman.

(Image credit: Alastair Grant)
item.dc:creator: Robbie Griffiths

item.title: Thousands of U.S. troops deploy to Middle East. And, the latest on DHS funding talks
item.description: The U.S. is sending thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne to the Middle East. And, congressional Republicans present Democrats with a new deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. is sending thousands of paratroopers from the 82nd Airborne to the Middle East. And, congressional Republicans present Democrats with a new deal to fund the Department of Homeland Security.

(Image credit: Alex Wong)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Iran rejects Trump's proposal to end the war and lays out 5 conditions
item.description: Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.
item.content:encoded:
Iran says no to President Trump's ceasefire plan and publicized a counterproposal that includes war reparations and Iranian sovereignty over the Strait of Hormuz.

(Image credit: Majid Saeedi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Islandwide blackouts bring life in Cuba to a near total halt
item.description: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez asks CNN's Havana Bureau Chief Patrick Oppmann about Cuba's power blackouts, which have brought the country to a near total halt.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: US and China both believe the other is a declining power
item.description: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.
item.content:encoded: China believes the U.S. is a declining power with expansionist ambitions. The U.S. thinks the same of China.
item.dc:creator: Emily Feng

item.title: Rubio plans travel to France to sell Iran war to skeptical G7 allies
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.
item.content:encoded:
Secretary of State Marco Rubio will travel to France this week to try to sell America's skeptical Group of Seven allies on the Iran war that has sent global fuel prices soaring.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
